A high-quality Saturday looms across Australia, with Group 1 features at Rosehill and Flemington, emerging 3-year-olds and seasoned campaigners converging in a meeting that blends depth and intrigue.

Overpass headlined a strong Randwick trial session on Friday, defeating classy individuals such as Jokers Grin and Tentyris, while a number of proven and promising 2-year-olds added depth to the card.

Ahead of the 2026 Inglis Easter Yearling Sale, we have identified four yearlings whose pedigrees have received black-type or metropolitan updates over the past couple of months, potentially increasing their value and certainly enhancing their profile.

An Extreme opportunity - Newgate Farm's Henry Field will make Extreme Choice available on Northern Hemisphere time, stating that “every major international organisation” has the statistical freak on their radar.

Just days after his victory in the G1 Golden Slipper Stakes, Coolmore Stud have moved to secure Guest House for a reported $30 million to stand beside his sire Home Affairs at Jerrys Plains at the conclusion of his racing career.

The Inglis Australian Easter Yearling Sale was where Coolmore Stud purchased their leading first season sire Home Affairs, and it is where they return year on year with the same intention; acquire the best horses possible, so they can offer the best-bred horses possible in years to come.

Dual Group 3-winning juvenile Espionage will join the Lovatsville roster in 2026 at a fee of $11,000 (inc GST). Following the blueprint of successful stud farms before him, Sam White shares how the son of Zoustar brings something different to Victorian breeders.
The entries are open for the 2026 Les Young Scholarship, which offers subsidised travel and accommodation for the recipient to enjoy hands-on study at the prestigious National Stud in Newmarket.
